problem_statement: Sequence transduction systems were limited by recurrent computation and poor parallelism.
key_insight: Stacked multi-head self-attention plus positional encoding captures long-range dependencies while enabling efficient parallel training.

contributions.novelty: Eliminates recurrence and convolutions in the core sequence model.

contributions.claim: Uses multi-head attention to model different representation subspaces.
contributions.novelty: Splits attention into several learned heads with a final projection.

requirements.name: PyTorch
requirements.notes: Recommended framework for the reference implementation.

requirements.name: WMT14 En-De
requirements.notes: Primary translation benchmark used in the paper.

concept_nodes.label: Encoder
concept_nodes.description: Stack of self-attention and feed-forward blocks.

concept_nodes.label: Decoder
concept_nodes.description: Autoregressive stack with masked self-attention and encoder-decoder attention.

concept_nodes.label: Multi-Head Attention
concept_nodes.description: Parallel attention heads over projected inputs.

concept_nodes.label: Positional Encoding
concept_nodes.description: Injects token position information into embeddings.

concept_nodes.label: WMT14
concept_nodes.description: Machine translation benchmark used for evaluation.

core_algorithm_description: Embed source and target tokens, add positional encodings, pass them through encoder and decoder stacks with multi-head attention, then project decoder states to vocabulary logits for training and decoding.
input_output_spec: Accepts tokenized source and target sequences and returns next-token logits over the target vocabulary.
evaluation_protocol: Train on WMT14 translation data and report BLEU on English-to-German and English-to-French benchmarks.
